text.title: AI Design System Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Semantic/primitive token confusion, deprecated Storybook API, interaction state gaps, large codebase chunking, and documentation accuracy traps.

antipattern.description: AI confuses semantic tokens (--color-action-primary) with primitive tokens (--color-blue-500) in suggestions.
antipattern.reason: Without explicit semantic/primitive distinction in the prompt, agents use the terms interchangeably. Semantic tokens must reference primitive tokens — not the reverse. Require explicit token layer distinction in every token-related prompt.

antipattern.description: AI-generated Storybook stories use deprecated CSF 2 syntax instead of CSF 3.
antipattern.reason: Without specifying the Storybook version in the prompt, agents default to CSF 2 patterns that generate deprecation warnings or fail in Storybook 8+. Always specify the exact Storybook and CSF version.

antipattern.description: AI-generated documentation not reviewed by the engineer who built the component.
antipattern.reason: AI documentation reads accurately but frequently contains subtle errors about edge cases, deprecated props, or platform-specific behavior. Only the component author can verify these details.

antipattern.description: Running token inconsistency detection on a codebase with more than 500 files in a single prompt.
antipattern.reason: Large codebases exceed single-prompt context. Chunk by directory; aggregate and de-duplicate results after all chunks are processed.

antipattern.description: Creating new token names in AI-generated documentation without human approval.
antipattern.reason: AI invents token names that may duplicate existing tokens or violate naming conventions. Require the agent to reference existing token names only; flag any proposed new token for human review.

antipattern.description: Using AI to build foundational design system structure from scratch.
antipattern.reason: AI amplifies existing structure; it does not create coherent foundations. A system built from AI output will have inconsistent token relationships, arbitrary naming, and no coherent design rationale. Human-led foundation design must come first.
